v.CNV: 我又看见日光之下所发生一切欺压的事：受欺压的流泪，却无人安慰他们；欺压他们的，手里握着权柄，因此无人安慰受欺压的。
v.ESV: Again I saw all the oppressions that are done under the sun. And behold, the tears of the oppressed, and they had no one to comfort them! On the side of their oppressors there was power, and there was no one to comfort them.
v.GAE: 내가 다시 해 아래에서 행하는 모든 학대를 살펴 보았도다 보라 학대 받는 자들의 눈물이로다 그들에게 위로자가 없도다 그들을 학대하는 자들의 손에는 권세가 있으나 그들에게는 위로자가 없도다
v.KJV: So I returned, and considered all the oppressions that are done under the sun: and behold the tears of such as were oppressed, and they had no comforter; and on the side of their oppressors there was power; but they had no comforter.
v.NASB: Then I looked again at all the acts of oppression which were being done under the sun. And behold I saw the tears of the oppressed and that they had no one to comfort them; and on the side of their oppressors was power, but they had no one to comfort them.
v.NIV: Again I looked and saw all the oppression that was taking place under the sun: I saw the tears of the oppressed— and they have no comforter; power was on the side of their oppressors— and they have no comforter.
v.NRSV: Again I saw all the oppressions that are practiced under the sun. Look, the tears of the oppressed — with no one to comfort them! On the side of their oppressors there was power — with no one to comfort them.
v.SAENEW: 나는 또 세상에서 벌어지는 온갖 억압을 보았다. 억눌리는 사람들이 눈물을 흘려도, 그들을 위로하는 사람이 없다. 억누르는 사람들은 폭력을 휘두르는데, 억눌리는 사람들을 위로하는 사람이 없다.
v.WLB: 내가 다시 해 아래에서 일어나고 있는 모든 억압당하는 일을 살펴보았다. 억압받는 사람들이 눈물을 흘리지만 그들을 위로해 줄 사람이 없다. 억압하는 사람들은 힘을 행사하는데 억압받는 사람들을 위로하는 사람이 없다.

v.CNV: 我赞叹那已死的人，胜过那还活着的人。
v.ESV: And I thought the dead who are already dead more fortunate than the living who are still alive.
v.GAE: 그러므로 나는 아직 살아 있는 산 자들보다 죽은 지 오랜 죽은 자들을 더 복되다 하였으며
v.KJV: Wherefore I praised the dead which are already dead more than the living which are yet alive.
v.NASB: So I congratulated the dead who are already dead more than the living who are still living.
v.NIV: And I declared that the dead, who had already died, are happier than the living, who are still alive.
v.NRSV: And I thought the dead, who have already died, more fortunate than the living, who are still alive;
v.SAENEW: 그래서 나는, 아직 살아 숨쉬는 사람보다는, 이미 숨이 넘어가 죽은 사람이 더 복되다고 말하였다.
v.WLB: 그러므로 이미 죽은 사람들이 아직 살아 있는 사람들보다 복이 있다는 생각이 들었다.

v.CNV: 那还没生下来的，就是还没看过日光之下所行的恶事的，比这两种人更有福。
v.ESV: But better than both is he who has not yet been and has not seen the evil deeds that are done under the sun.
v.GAE: 이 둘보다도 아직 출생하지 아니하여 해 아래에서 행하는 악한 일을 보지 못한 자가 더 복되다 하였노라
v.KJV: Yea, better is he than both they, which hath not yet been, who hath not seen the evil work that is done under the sun.
v.NASB: But better off than both of them is the one who has never existed, who has never seen the evil activity that is done under the sun.
v.NIV: But better than both is the one who has never been born, who has not seen the evil that is done under the sun.
v.NRSV: but better than both is the one who has not yet been, and has not seen the evil deeds that are done under the sun.
v.SAENEW: 그리고 이 둘보다는, 아직 태어나지 않아서 세상에서 저질러지는 온갖 못된 일을 못 본 사람이 더 낫다고 하였다.
v.WLB: 그러나 이들보다 더 복이 있는 사람은 아직 태어나지 않아서 해 아래에서 자행된 온갖 악을 보지 못한 사람이다.

v.CNV: 我看见各样的劳碌和各样精巧的工作，都是出于人与人彼此的竞争。这也是虚空，也是捕风。
v.ESV: Then I saw that all toil and all skill in work come from a man's envy of his neighbor. This also is vanity and a striving after wind.
v.GAE: 내가 또 본즉 사람이 모든 수고와 모든 재주로 말미암아 이웃에게 시기를 받으니 이것도 헛되어 바람을 잡는 것이로다
v.KJV: Again, I considered all travail, and every right work, that for this a man is envied of his neighbour. This is also vanity and vexation of spirit.
v.NASB: I have seen that every labor and every skill which is done is the result of rivalry between a man and his neighbor. This too is vanity and striving after wind.
v.NIV: And I saw that all toil and all achievement spring from one person’s envy of another. This too is meaningless, a chasing after the wind.
v.NRSV: Then I saw that all toil and all skill in work come from one person’s envy of another. This also is vanity and a chasing after wind.
v.SAENEW: 온갖 노력과 성취는 바로 사람끼리 갖는 경쟁심에서 비롯되는 것임을 나는 깨달았다. 그러나 이 수고도 헛되고, 바람을 잡으려는 것과 같다.
v.WLB: 또 나는 모든 노력과 모든 성취가 결국 이웃을 시기하는 마음에서 비롯된 것임을 깨달았다. 이것 또한 허무하고 뜬구름 잡는 일이다.

v.CNV: 愚昧人抱着手，吃自己的肉。
v.ESV: The fool folds his hands and eats his own flesh.
v.GAE: 우매자는 팔짱을 끼고 있으면서 자기의 몸만 축내는도다
v.KJV: The fool foldeth his hands together, and eateth his own flesh.
v.NASB: The fool folds his hands and consumes his own flesh.
v.NIV: Fools fold their hands and ruin themselves.
v.NRSV: Fools fold their hands and consume their own flesh.
v.SAENEW: "어리석은 사람은 팔짱을 끼고 앉아서, 제 몸만 축낸다"고 하지만,
v.WLB: 어리석은 사람은 팔짱을 끼고는 자기 몸만 축내는구나.

v.CNV: 一掌盛满安宁，胜过两手抓满劳碌捕风。
v.ESV: Better is a handful of quietness than two hands full of toil and a striving after wind.
v.GAE: 두 손에 가득하고 수고하며 바람을 잡는 것보다 한 손에만 가득하고 평온함이 더 나으니라
v.KJV: Better is an handful with quietness, than both the hands full with travail and vexation of spirit.
v.NASB: One hand full of rest is better than two fists full of labor and striving after wind.
v.NIV: Better one handful with tranquillity than two handfuls with toil and chasing after the wind.
v.NRSV: Better is a handful with quiet than two handfuls with toil, and a chasing after wind.
v.SAENEW: 적게 가지고 편안한 것이, 많이 가지려고 수고하며 바람을 잡는 것보다 낫다.
v.WLB: 한 손에만 가득하고 평안한 것이 양손에 가득하고 고통과 번뇌가 있는 것보다 낫다.

v.CNV: 我又看到日光之下有一件虚空的事。
v.ESV: Again, I saw vanity under the sun:
v.GAE: 내가 또 다시 해 아래에서 헛된 것을 보았도다
v.KJV: Then I returned, and I saw vanity under the sun.
v.NASB: Then I looked again at vanity under the sun.
v.NIV: Again I saw something meaningless under the sun:
v.NRSV: Again, I saw vanity under the sun:
v.SAENEW: 나는 세상에서 헛된 것을 또 보았다.
v.WLB: 내가 또 돌이켜 해 아래에서 허무한 것들을 보았다.

v.CNV: 有人孤单无依，没有儿子，没有兄弟，仍劳碌不休，眼目也不以自己的财富为足。他问：“我劳劳碌碌，刻薄自己不去享受，是为谁呢？”这也是虚空，是劳苦的担子。
v.ESV: one person who has no other, either son or brother, yet there is no end to all his toil, and his eyes are never satisfied with riches, so that he never asks, “For whom am I toiling and depriving myself of pleasure?” This also is vanity and an unhappy business.
v.GAE: 어떤 사람은 아들도 없고 형제도 없이 홀로 있으나 그의 모든 수고에는 끝이 없도다 또 비록 그의 눈은 부요를 족하게 여기지 아니하면서 이르기를 내가 누구를 위하여는 이같이 수고하고 나를 위하여는 행복을 누리지 못하게 하는가 하여도 이것도 헛되어 불행한 노고로다
v.KJV: There is one alone, and there is not a second; yea, he hath neither child nor brother: yet is there no end of all his labour; neither is his eye satisfied with riches; neither saith he, For whom do I labour, and bereave my soul of good? This is also vanity, yea, it is a sore travail.
v.NASB: There was a certain man without a dependent, having neither a son nor a brother, yet there was no end to all his labor. Indeed, his eyes were not satisfied with riches and he never asked, 'And for whom am I laboring and depriving myself of pleasure?' This too is vanity and it is a grievous task.
v.NIV: There was a man all alone; he had neither son nor brother. There was no end to his toil, yet his eyes were not content with his wealth. “For whom am I toiling,” he asked, “and why am I depriving myself of enjoyment?” This too is meaningless— a miserable business!
v.NRSV: the case of solitary individuals, without sons or brothers; yet there is no end to all their toil, and their eyes are never satisfied with riches. “For whom am I toiling,” they ask, “and depriving myself of pleasure?” This also is vanity and an unhappy business.
v.SAENEW: 한 남자가 있다. 자식도 형제도 없이 혼자 산다. 그러나 그는 쉬지도 않고 일만 하며 산다. 그렇게 해서 모은 재산도 그의 눈에는 차지 않는다. 그러면서도 그는 가끔, "어찌하여 나는 즐기지도 못하고 사는가? 도대체 내가 누구 때문에 이 수고를 하는가?" 하고 말하니, 그의 수고도 헛되고, 부질없는 일이다.
v.WLB: 혈혈단신으로 아들도 형제도 없는 사람이 있는데 그는 끝도 없이 열심히 수고한다. 그러나 쌓여 가는 재물도 그의 눈에는 도무지 흡족하지 않고 “내가 누구를 위해 이렇게 열심히 일하나? 내 영혼이 왜 이렇게 누리지도 못하고 있나?”라고 말하지도 않으니 이것 또한 허무하고 비참한 일이다.

v.CNV: 二人胜过一人，因为他们一起的劳碌有美好的酬报。
v.ESV: Two are better than one, because they have a good reward for their toil.
v.GAE: 두 사람이 한 사람보다 나음은 그들이 수고함으로 좋은 상을 얻을 것임이라
v.KJV: Two are better than one; because they have a good reward for their labour.
v.NASB: Two are better than one because they have a good return for their labor.
v.NIV: Two are better than one, because they have a good return for their labor:
v.NRSV: Two are better than one, because they have a good reward for their toil.
v.SAENEW: 혼자보다는 둘이 더 낫다. 두 사람이 함께 일할 때에, 더 좋은 결과를 얻을 수 있기 때문이다.
v.WLB: 하나보다 둘이 더 낫다. 둘이 함께 노력하면 더 좋은 결과를 얻기 때문이다.

v.CNV: 如果一个跌倒，另一个可以把他的同伴扶起来。但一人孤身跌倒，没有别人把他扶起来，他就悲惨了。
v.ESV: For if they fall, one will lift up his fellow. But woe to him who is alone when he falls and has not another to lift him up!
v.GAE: 혹시 그들이 넘어지면 하나가 그 동무를 붙들어 일으키려니와 홀로 있어 넘어지고 붙들어 일으킬 자가 없는 자에게는 화가 있으리라
v.KJV: For if they fall, the one will lift up his fellow: but woe to him that is alone when he falleth; for he hath not another to help him up.
v.NASB: For if either of them falls, the one will lift up his companion. But woe to the one who falls when there is not another to lift him up.
v.NIV: If either of them falls down, one can help the other up. But pity anyone who falls and has no one to help them up.
v.NRSV: For if they fall, one will lift up the other; but woe to one who is alone and falls and does not have another to help.
v.SAENEW: 그 가운데 하나가 넘어지면, 다른 한 사람이 자기의 동무를 일으켜 줄 수 있다. 그러나 혼자 가다가 넘어지면, 딱하게도, 일으켜 줄 사람이 없다.
v.WLB: 넘어지게 되면 하나가 다른 하나를 일으켜 줄 수 있다. 그러나 혼자여서 넘어져도 일으켜 줄 사람이 없으면 얼마나 불쌍한가!

v.CNV: 还有，二人同睡，就都暖和；一人独睡，怎能暖和呢？
v.ESV: Again, if two lie together, they keep warm, but how can one keep warm alone?
v.GAE: 또 두 사람이 함께 누우면 따뜻하거니와 한 사람이면 어찌 따뜻하랴
v.KJV: Again, if two lie together, then they have heat: but how can one be warm alone?
v.NASB: Furthermore, if two lie down together they keep warm, but how can one be warm alone?
v.NIV: Also, if two lie down together, they will keep warm. But how can one keep warm alone?
v.NRSV: Again, if two lie together, they keep warm; but how can one keep warm alone?
v.SAENEW: 또 둘이 누우면 따뜻하지만, 혼자라면 어찌 따뜻하겠는가?
v.WLB: 또 둘이 함께 누우면 따뜻해지지만 혼자라면 어떻게 따뜻해지겠는가?

v.CNV: 有人能制伏孤身一人，如果有二人就挡得住他。三股合成的绳子，不容易扯断。
v.ESV: And though a man might prevail against one who is alone, two will withstand him—a threefold cord is not quickly broken.
v.GAE: 한 사람이면 패하겠거니와 두 사람이면 맞설 수 있나니 세 겹 줄은 쉽게 끊어지지 아니하느니라
v.KJV: And if one prevail against him, two shall withstand him; and a threefold cord is not quickly broken.
v.NASB: And if one can overpower him who is alone, two can resist him. A cord of three strands is not quickly torn apart.
v.NIV: Though one may be overpowered, two can defend themselves. A cord of three strands is not quickly broken.
v.NRSV: And though one might prevail against another, two will withstand one. A threefold cord is not quickly broken.
v.SAENEW: 혼자 싸우면 지지만, 둘이 힘을 합하면 적에게 맞설 수 있다. 세 겹 줄은 쉽게 끊어지지 않는다.
v.WLB: 혼자서는 질 일도 둘이서는 당해 낼 수 있으니 세 겹 줄은 쉽게 끊어지지 않는다.

v.CNV: 贫穷但有智慧的年轻人，胜过年老不再纳谏的愚昧王。
v.ESV: Better was a poor and wise youth than an old and foolish king who no longer knew how to take advice.
v.GAE: 가난하여도 지혜로운 젊은이가 늙고 둔하여 경고를 더 받을 줄 모르는 왕보다 나으니
v.KJV: Better is a poor and a wise child than an old and foolish king, who will no more be admonished.
v.NASB: A poor yet wise lad is better than an old and foolish king who no longer knows how to receive instruction.
v.NIV: Better a poor but wise youth than an old but foolish king who no longer knows how to heed a warning.
v.NRSV: Better is a poor but wise youth than an old but foolish king, who will no longer take advice.
v.SAENEW: 아무리 나이가 많아도 신하의 직언을 듣지 않는 왕은 어리석다. 그보다는 가난할지라도 슬기로운 젊은이가 더 낫다.
v.WLB: 남의 말을 받아들일 줄 모르는 늙고 어리석은 왕보다는 가난해도 지혜로운 청년이 백 번 낫다.

v.CNV: 虽然他是从监狱出来，在自己的国中，又是出身贫寒，却起来作王。
v.ESV: For he went from prison to the throne, though in his own kingdom he had been born poor.
v.GAE: 그는 자기의 나라에서 가난하게 태어났을지라도 감옥에서 나와 왕이 되었음이니라
v.KJV: For out of prison he cometh to reign; whereas also he that is born in his kingdom becometh poor.
v.NASB: For he has come out of prison to become king, even though he was born poor in his kingdom.
v.NIV: The youth may have come from prison to the kingship, or he may have been born in poverty within his kingdom.
v.NRSV: One can indeed come out of prison to reign, even though born poor in the kingdom.
v.SAENEW: 한 나라의 가난한 집안에서 태어나서 젊어서 감옥살이를 하다가도 임금자리에 오를 수 있다.
v.WLB: 그런 청년은 감옥에 있다가도 나와서 다스릴 수 있다. 그가 그 나라에서 가난하게 태어났어도 말이다.

v.CNV: 我看见所有在日光之下行走的活人，都随从那取代老王的年轻人。
v.ESV: I saw all the living who move about under the sun, along with that youth who was to stand in the king's place.
v.GAE: 내가 본즉 해 아래에서 다니는 인생들이 왕의 다음 자리에 있다가 왕을 대신하여 일어난 젊은이와 함께 있고
v.KJV: I considered all the living which walk under the sun, with the second child that shall stand up in his stead.
v.NASB: I have seen all the living under the sun throng to the side of the second lad who replaces him.
v.NIV: I saw that all who lived and walked under the sun followed the youth, the king’s successor.
v.NRSV: I saw all the living who, moving about under the sun, follow that youth who replaced the king;
v.SAENEW: 내가 보니, 세상에서 살아 움직이는 모든 사람이, 왕의 후계자가 된 젊은이를 따른다.
v.WLB: 해 아래 다니는 모든 사람이 왕의 후계자가 된 그 청년을 따랐다.

v.CNV: 所有的人民，就是他所统治的人民，多得无数；然而后来的人却不喜欢他。这实在也是虚空，也是捕风。
v.ESV: There was no end of all the people, all of whom he led. Yet those who come later will not rejoice in him. Surely this also is vanity and a striving after wind.
v.GAE: 그의 치리를 받는 모든 백성들이 무수하였을지라도 후에 오는 자들은 그를 기뻐하지 아니하리니 이것도 헛되어 바람을 잡는 것이로다
v.KJV: There is no end of all the people, even of all that have been before them: they also that come after shall not rejoice in him. Surely this also is vanity and vexation of spirit.
v.NASB: There is no end to all the people, to all who were before them, and even the ones who will come later will not be happy with him, for this too is vanity and striving after wind.
v.NIV: There was no end to all the people who were before them. But those who came later were not pleased with the successor. This too is meaningless, a chasing after the wind.
v.NRSV: there was no end to all those people whom he led. Yet those who come later will not rejoice in him. Surely this also is vanity and a chasing after wind.
v.SAENEW: 한 왕이 다스리는 백성의 수가 셀 수 없이 많다 하여도, 그가 물러나면 어느 누구도 그의 업적을 찬양하지 않으니, 왕으로서 통치하는 것도 헛되며 바람을 잡으려는 것과 다를 바 없다.
v.WLB: 이게 모든 무수한 백성들의 속성이니 그 이전 세대도 마찬가지였다. 그 다음 세대는 그를 기뻐하지 않을 것이다. 이것 또한 허무하고 뜬구름 잡는 일이다.
